name: Fortuna
title: Roman - Fortuna
description: Also known as Fortuna Primigenia. Associated with unknown.

keyMyths.title: The Oracle at Praeneste
keyMyths.description: Fortuna Primigenia's oracle at Praeneste was consulted using inscribed oak slips. A young boy would draw a slip, revealing the prophecy written on it, showcasing Fortuna's ability to reveal the future.

keyMyths.title: Servius Tullius's Favor
keyMyths.description: The future king Servius Tullius, as a child, was said to have had his head burst into flames while he slept. Tanaquil, wife of Tarquinius Priscus, interpreted this as a sign of Fortuna's favor, leading to his rise to power.

keyMyths.title: Fortuna and the Ship of Grain
keyMyths.description: During a grain shortage, a ship laden with grain miraculously arrived in Rome. This was attributed to Fortuna's intervention, demonstrating her control over prosperity and abundance.

keyMyths.title: The Founding of the Temple of Fortuna Muliebris
keyMyths.description: The Temple of Fortuna Muliebris was founded to commemorate the actions of the Roman women who persuaded Coriolanus to lift his siege of Rome, illustrating Fortuna's connection to female virtue and the city's safety.
keyMyths.source: Valerius Maximus, Facta et Dicta Memorabilia 1.8.4

keyMyths.title: Fortuna Annonaria and the Grain Supply
keyMyths.description: Fortuna Annonaria, a specific aspect of Fortuna, was associated with the grain supply of Rome. Temples were dedicated to her to ensure a stable and plentiful food supply for the city's population.

sources.author: Cicero
sources.description: Philosophical treatise discussing divination and prophecy, including the oracle of Fortuna Primigenia.
sources.text: Philosophical treatise discussing divination and prophecy, including the oracle of Fortuna Primigenia.

sources.author: Livy
sources.description: Monumental history of Rome, mentioning Fortuna's role in the rise of Servius Tullius.
sources.text: Monumental history of Rome, mentioning Fortuna's role in the rise of Servius Tullius.

sources.author: Pliny the Elder
sources.description: Encyclopedic work covering various aspects of the natural world, including references to Fortuna's influence on agriculture and prosperity.
sources.text: Encyclopedic work covering various aspects of the natural world, including references to Fortuna's influence on agriculture and prosperity.

sources.author: Valerius Maximus
sources.description: Collection of historical anecdotes and moral exempla, including the story of the Temple of Fortuna Muliebris.
sources.text: Collection of historical anecdotes and moral exempla, including the story of the Temple of Fortuna Muliebris.

sources.author: Inscriptiones Latinae Selectae
sources.description: A collection of Latin inscriptions, including dedications to Fortuna Annonaria related to the grain supply.
sources.text: A collection of Latin inscriptions, including dedications to Fortuna Annonaria related to the grain supply.
